title: torilis elongata
intro: Torilis elongata, also known as wild chervil or hemlock parsley, is a biennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Torilis elongata include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Torilis elongata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
